Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotConnector

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotElement
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotConnector
All Implemented Interfaces:
IPotElement
public class PotConnector extends PotElement
Represents a connector shape that can be drawn between points or shapes on a slide.

A connector is a line that can be used to link elements visually, such as in flowcharts or diagrams. It supports various line styles, arrowheads, and connection points to other shapes. The connector's geometry is defined by a bounding rectangle where the start and end points are determined by the rectangle's corners and flip properties.

Usage Example: Creating a Simple Connector


 // Create a straight connector from (100,100) to (300,200)
 PotConnector line = slide.addConnector(PotConnector.ConnectorType.STRAIGHT)
     .from(100, 100)
     .to(300, 200)
     .lineColor(PotColor.BLACK)
     .lineWidth(2);

 // Add arrowheads to both ends
 line.startArrow(PotConnector.ArrowType.TRIANGLE)
     .endArrow(PotConnector.ArrowType.TRIANGLE);

Usage Example: Connecting Two Shapes


 // Create a connector that automatically links shape1 to shape2
 PotConnector connector = slide.addConnector(shape1, shape2);
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    PotConnector.ArrowSize
    Defines the relative size of an arrowhead.
    static enum 
    PotConnector.ArrowType
    Defines the style of arrowheads that can be applied to the connector ends.
    static enum 
    PotConnector.ConnectorType
    Defines the visual style of the connector line.

  • Method Details

    • getConnectorType

      public PotConnector.ConnectorType getConnectorType()
      Returns the visual type of this connector.
      Returns:
      the current connector type, never null
      Since:
      1.0

    • setConnectorType

      public PotConnector setConnectorType(PotConnector.ConnectorType type)
      Sets the visual type of this connector and updates the underlying XML.

      The connector type determines the line geometry: straight, elbow, or curved. If the provided type is null, it defaults to PotConnector.ConnectorType.STRAIGHT.

      Parameters:
      type - the new connector type, or null to use the default
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • from

      public PotConnector from(double x, double y)
      Sets the starting point of the connector, preserving the current end point.

      This method adjusts the connector's anchor rectangle so that its effective start point becomes the specified coordinates. The end point remains at its current location relative to the new anchor.

      Parameters:
      x - the X-coordinate of the start point, in points
      y - the Y-coordinate of the start point, in points
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • to

      public PotConnector to(double x, double y)
      Sets the ending point of the connector, preserving the current start point.

      This method adjusts the connector's anchor rectangle so that its effective end point becomes the specified coordinates. The start point remains at its current location relative to the new anchor.

      Parameters:
      x - the X-coordinate of the end point, in points
      y - the Y-coordinate of the end point, in points
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • line

      public PotConnector line(double x1, double y1, double x2, double y2)
      Sets both the start and end points of the connector in a single call.

      This method defines the connector's bounding rectangle using the minimum and maximum coordinates. The start point is (x1, y1) and the end point is (x2, y2). The connector's flip properties are automatically set if the end point coordinates are less than the start point coordinates.

      Parameters:
      x1 - the X-coordinate of the start point, in points
      y1 - the Y-coordinate of the start point, in points
      x2 - the X-coordinate of the end point, in points
      y2 - the Y-coordinate of the end point, in points
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • getStartX

      public double getStartX()
      Returns the X-coordinate of the connector's start point.

      The start point is determined by the anchor rectangle and the horizontal flip state. If the connector is flipped horizontally, the start point is at the right edge; otherwise, it is at the left edge.

      Returns:
      the X-coordinate of the start point, in points, or 0 if the anchor is not set
      Since:
      1.0

    • getStartY

      public double getStartY()
      Returns the Y-coordinate of the connector's start point.

      The start point is determined by the anchor rectangle and the vertical flip state. If the connector is flipped vertically, the start point is at the bottom edge; otherwise, it is at the top edge.

      Returns:
      the Y-coordinate of the start point, in points, or 0 if the anchor is not set
      Since:
      1.0

    • getEndX

      public double getEndX()
      Returns the X-coordinate of the connector's end point.

      The end point is determined by the anchor rectangle and the horizontal flip state. If the connector is flipped horizontally, the end point is at the left edge; otherwise, it is at the right edge.

      Returns:
      the X-coordinate of the end point, in points, or 0 if the anchor is not set
      Since:
      1.0

    • getEndY

      public double getEndY()
      Returns the Y-coordinate of the connector's end point.

      The end point is determined by the anchor rectangle and the vertical flip state. If the connector is flipped vertically, the end point is at the top edge; otherwise, it is at the bottom edge.

      Returns:
      the Y-coordinate of the end point, in points, or 0 if the anchor is not set
      Since:
      1.0

    • lineColor

      public PotConnector lineColor(PotColor color)
      Sets the line color using a PotColor.

      If the provided color is null, no change is made to the current line color.

      Parameters:
      color - the new line color, or null to leave unchanged
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • lineColor

      public PotConnector lineColor(Color color)
      Sets the line color using a standard AWT Color.
      Parameters:
      color - the new line color
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • lineWidth

      public PotConnector lineWidth(double width)
      Sets the line width.
      Parameters:
      width - the new line width, in points
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • lineStyle

      public PotConnector lineStyle(PotBorder border)
      Applies a complete border style to the connector line.

      This method sets the line color, width, dash style, and cap style from the provided PotBorder object. Any null properties in the border are ignored.

      Parameters:
      border - the border style to apply
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • dash

      public PotConnector dash(PotBorder.DashStyle dashStyle)
      Sets the dash style of the connector line.

      If the provided dash style is null, no change is made to the current dash style.

      Parameters:
      dashStyle - the new dash style, or null to leave unchanged
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • getLineColor

      public PotColor getLineColor()
      Returns the current line color as a PotColor.
      Returns:
      the line color, or null if no color is set
      Since:
      1.0

    • getLineWidth

      public double getLineWidth()
      Returns the current line width.
      Returns:
      the line width, in points
      Since:
      1.0

    • startArrow

      public PotConnector startArrow(PotConnector.ArrowType arrowType)
      Sets the arrowhead style at the start of the connector.

      If the provided arrow type is null, the start arrow remains unchanged. The arrowhead is drawn at the line's start point, which is determined by the connector's geometry and flip state.

      Parameters:
      arrowType - the arrowhead style for the start of the connector
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • startArrow

      public PotConnector startArrow(PotConnector.ArrowType arrowType, PotConnector.ArrowSize width, PotConnector.ArrowSize length)
      Sets the arrowhead style and dimensions at the start of the connector.

      This method allows specifying both the arrowhead shape and its size. If the provided arrow type is null, the start arrow remains unchanged. The width and length parameters control the arrowhead's proportions.

      Parameters:
      arrowType - the arrowhead style for the start of the connector
      width - the width (crosssection) of the arrowhead
      length - the length (tiptobase) of the arrowhead
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • endArrow

      public PotConnector endArrow(PotConnector.ArrowType arrowType)
      Sets the arrowhead style at the end of the connector.

      If the provided arrow type is null, the end arrow remains unchanged. The arrowhead is drawn at the line's end point, which is determined by the connector's geometry and flip state.

      Parameters:
      arrowType - the arrowhead style for the end of the connector
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • endArrow

      public PotConnector endArrow(PotConnector.ArrowType arrowType, PotConnector.ArrowSize width, PotConnector.ArrowSize length)
      Sets the arrowhead style and dimensions at the end of the connector.

      This method allows specifying both the arrowhead shape and its size. If the provided arrow type is null, the end arrow remains unchanged. The width and length parameters control the arrowhead's proportions.

      Parameters:
      arrowType - the arrowhead style for the end of the connector
      width - the width (crosssection) of the arrowhead
      length - the length (tiptobase) of the arrowhead
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • noArrow

      public PotConnector noArrow()
      Removes arrowheads from both ends of the connector.

      This method sets both the start and end arrowheads to PotConnector.ArrowType.NONE.

      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • connectFrom

      public PotConnector connectFrom(PotElement element)
      Connects the start of this connector to another shape element.

      This method establishes a visual link from the start point of this connector to the specified shape. The connection is stored in the underlying XML as a stCxn element. If the element is null, no connection is made.

      Parameters:
      element - the shape to connect from
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • connectTo

      public PotConnector connectTo(PotElement element)
      Connects the end of this connector to another shape element.

      This method establishes a visual link from the end point of this connector to the specified shape. The connection is stored in the underlying XML as an endCxn element. If the element is null, no connection is made.

      Parameters:
      element - the shape to connect to
      Returns:
      this connector instance for method chaining
      Since:
      1.0

    • duplicate

      public PotConnector duplicate()
      Creates a duplicate of this connector on the same slide.

      The duplicate is a new connector with the same geometry, line style, and arrowheads. The copy is placed at the same position and retains the same visual appearance. The underlying shape ID and UUID are different from the original.

      Usage Example

      
 PotConnector original = slide.addConnector(ConnectorType.ELBOW)
     .from(50, 50).to(200, 150)
     .lineColor(PotColor.RED);
 PotConnector copy = original.duplicate();
      Specified by:
      duplicate in interface IPotElement
      Overrides:
      duplicate in class PotElement
      Returns:
      a new PotConnector instance that is a copy of this one
      Since:
      1.0

    • getRawConnectorShape

      public org.apache.poi.xslf.usermodel.XSLFConnectorShape getRawConnectorShape()
      Returns the underlying Apache POI connector shape.

      This method provides direct access to the internal XSLFConnectorShape for advanced operations not covered by the PotShaper API. Use with caution, as modifications may conflict with PotShaper's internal state.

      Returns:
      the raw Apache POI XSLFConnectorShape
      Since:
      1.0

      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Scales the element uniformly by the specified factor.

      Parameters:

      public PotConnector scale(double scaleX, double scaleY)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Scales the element independently along the X and Y axes.

      Parameters:
      scaleX - the horizontal scaling factor; must be positive
      scaleY - the vertical scaling factor; must be positive
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotate

      public PotConnector rotate(double angle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the rotation angle of the element.

      Rotation is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      rotate in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      angle - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotation

      public PotConnector rotation(double angle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the rotation angle of the element (fluent builder alias).
      Overrides:
      rotation in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      angle - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotateBy

      public PotConnector rotateBy(double deltaAngle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Rotates the element by the specified angle delta.

      Rotation is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      rotateBy in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      deltaAngle - the angle to add to the current rotation, in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • flipHorizontal

      public PotConnector flipHorizontal()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Toggles the horizontal flip state of the element.

      Flipping is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      flipHorizontal in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • flipVertical

      public PotConnector flipVertical()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Toggles the vertical flip state of the element.

      Flipping is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:

      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • bringToFront

      public PotConnector bringToFront()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Brings this element to the front of the slide's drawing order.

      This operation manipulates the underlying XML structure of the slide.

      Overrides:
      bringToFront in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • sendToBack

      public PotConnector sendToBack()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sends this element to the back of the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      sendToBack in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • bringForward

      public PotConnector bringForward()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Brings this element one step forward in the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      bringForward in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • sendBackward

      public PotConnector sendBackward()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sends this element one step backward in the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      sendBackward in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • animate

      public PotConnector animate(PotAnimation animation)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies an animation to this element.
      Overrides:
      animate in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      animation - the animation to apply; must not be null
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeAnimation

      public PotConnector removeAnimation()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any animation attached to this element.
      Overrides:
      removeAnimation in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlink

      public PotConnector hyperlink(String url)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches a hyperlink to this element that points to an external URL.

      Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlink in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      url - the destination URL; must not be null
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlink

      public PotConnector hyperlink(PotHyperlink link)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches a hyperlink defined by a PotHyperlink object.

      If the provided link is null, any existing hyperlink is removed. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlink in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      link - the hyperlink configuration, or null to remove the hyperlink
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlinkToSlide

      public PotConnector hyperlinkToSlide(int slideIndex)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Creates a hyperlink that jumps to a specific slide within the same presentation.

      This method sets both the internal slide reference and a fallback address string. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlinkToSlide in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      slideIndex - the zerobased index of the target slide
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeHyperlink

      public PotConnector removeHyperlink()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any hyperlink attached to this element.

      This method manipulates the underlying XML because Apache POI does not provide a direct removal API. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      removeHyperlink in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • action

      public PotConnector action(PotAction action)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches an action (e.g., mouseclick or mouseover) to the element.
      Overrides:
      action in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      action - the action configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeAction

      public PotConnector removeAction(boolean isHover)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes an action from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeAction in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      isHover - true to remove a mouseover action, false to remove a mouseclick action
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • shadow

      public PotConnector shadow(PotShadow shadow)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a shadow effect to the element.

      Shadows are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected. Passing null or a shadow of type PotShadow.Type.NONE removes any existing shadow.

      Overrides:
      shadow in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      shadow - the shadow configuration, or null to remove the shadow
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeShadow

      public PotConnector removeShadow()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any shadow effect from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeShadow in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • reflection

      public PotConnector reflection(PotEffect.Reflection reflection)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a reflection effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      reflection in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      reflection - the reflection configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • glow

      public PotConnector glow(PotEffect.Glow glow)

      Applies a glow effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      glow in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      glow - the glow configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • softEdge

      public PotConnector softEdge(PotEffect.SoftEdge softEdge)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a softedge effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      softEdge in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      softEdge - the softedge configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeEffects

      public PotConnector removeEffects()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes all visual effects (shadow, reflection, glow, softedge, 3D) from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeEffects in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a 3D rotation effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      rotation3D in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      rotation - the 3D rotation configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Applies a bevel effect to the element.
      Overrides:

      Parameters:

      public PotConnector material(Pot3DFormat.Material material)

      Applies a material effect to the element.
      Overrides:

      Parameters:

      Returns:

    • lighting

      public PotConnector lighting(Pot3DFormat.Lighting lighting)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a lighting effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      lighting in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      lighting - the lighting configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • opacity

      public PotConnector opacity(double opacity)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the opacity (transparency) of the element.

      Opacity is applied by manipulating the underlying XML, as Apache POI does not provide a direct API. The value must be between 0.0 (fully transparent) and 1.0 (fully opaque).

      Overrides:
      opacity in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      opacity - the opacity value between 0.0 (transparent) and 1.0 (opaque)
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this connector.

      The string includes the connector's UUID, type, and start/end coordinates.

      Overrides:
      toString in class PotElement
      Returns:
      a descriptive string representation
      Since:
      1.0
